name: Dental Records & HIPAA (ADA / state aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with ADA Principles of Ethics and HIPAA Privacy Rule. Tune retention to the state of practice (federal HIPAA minimum is 6 years).

data.text: Dental records & HIPAA — ADA / state aligned
Records are clear, contemporaneous, and detailed enough for another licensed dentist to continue care.
Patient records are retained per state law and at minimum for the HIPAA Privacy Rule period (typically 6 years).
Tooth charting uses Universal numbering (US standard); restorations, caries, mobility, and missing teeth are recorded at every comprehensive exam.
Periodontal status is recorded at every comprehensive exam — pocket depths, recession, BoP.
Treatment items are coded with current CDT codes.
Informed consent is documented for invasive procedures (extractions, endo, implants, sedation).
PHI disclosures, NPP acknowledgements, and BAAs with vendors are tracked and retained.

clauses.title: Records sufficient for handover
clauses.body: You must keep records detailed enough for another licensed dentist to continue care.

clauses.title: HIPAA / state retention
clauses.body: Records must be retained per state law and at minimum for the HIPAA Privacy Rule period (typically 6 years).

clauses.title: Tooth charting recorded
clauses.body: Every comprehensive exam must include tooth charting using Universal numbering.

clauses.title: Periodontal status recorded
clauses.body: Every comprehensive exam must record periodontal status (pocket depths, recession, BoP).

clauses.title: CDT coding used
clauses.body: Treatment items must be coded with current CDT codes.

clauses.title: Informed consent documented
clauses.body: Informed consent must be documented for invasive procedures.

clauses.title: HIPAA disclosures tracked
clauses.body: PHI disclosures, NPP acknowledgements, and BAAs must be tracked and retained.
